title: Post Fiat Canonical Price Resolution Policy
description: Deterministic rules for resolving a (symbol, timestamp) pair to a single canonical price. Eliminates private interpretation by defining source hierarchy, staleness, tolerance, interpolation, and dispute fallback.

source_hierarchy.description: Deterministic source precedence. Lower priority number = higher precedence. First available source within staleness window wins.

max_staleness_seconds.description: Per-source staleness override. Falls back to global if absent.

sources.description: Ordered list of price sources by priority

selection_rule.description: How to select among available sources. first_valid = highest-priority non-stale source. median_of_top_n = median of top N non-stale sources (reduces outlier risk). weighted_average = confidence-weighted mean.

top_n.description: Number of sources to consider for median_of_top_n or weighted_average

staleness.description: Maximum acceptable age of a price observation relative to the requested timestamp.

max_staleness_seconds.description: Global maximum staleness in seconds (default 7200 = 2 hours)

stale_action.description: What to do when primary source is stale: reject entirely, attempt interpolation, or try next source in hierarchy

tolerance.description: Price agreement tolerance when multiple sources are available.

max_deviation_percent.description: Maximum acceptable percentage deviation between sources before flagging a dispute

deviation_action.description: Action when sources deviate beyond tolerance

interpolation.description: Rules for interpolating price when no exact-timestamp observation exists.

method.description: Interpolation method. linear = time-weighted between neighbors. nearest = closest observation.

max_gap_seconds.description: Maximum gap between neighboring observations for interpolation to be valid (default 4 hours)

confidence_penalty.description: Confidence multiplier applied to interpolated prices (0.5 = halved confidence)

dispute.description: Fallback path when sources disagree beyond tolerance or all sources are stale.

fallback_chain.description: Ordered fallback steps. First successful step wins. mark_unresolved is terminal.

unresolved_action.description: What to do when the entire fallback chain exhausts without resolution
